name: Abhartach
description: Abhartach, often described as a dwarf or a malevolent chieftain, is a figure from Irish folklore said to have been a tyrannical ruler who was eventually slain. However, after being buried, he would rise again, demanding a bowl of blood. This cycle of death and resurrection continued until he was finally killed by Cathán, who pinned him down with a large stone and planted a rowan tree on top of him to prevent his return. The legend of Abhartach is linked to the idea of a vampire-like being who requires blood to sustain its unnatural existence. He is said to have possessed supernatural powers and was a source of terror to the people he ruled. The story serves as a cautionary tale about the dangers of unchecked power and the importance of overcoming evil. The location of his grave is often associated with a specific place name, adding a sense of historical reality to the myth. Some scholars believe that the legend of Abhartach may have influenced Bram Stoker's Dracula.
subtitle: The Irish Vampire King
appearance: Abhartach is typically depicted as a grotesque figure, often short in stature and physically deformed. Some accounts describe him as having long, sharp teeth and pale skin. His eyes are often described as being red or glowing, reflecting his unnatural state of existence. He is usually dressed in tattered or soiled garments, reflecting his burial and subsequent resurrections. Despite his small size, he possesses a menacing presence and exudes an aura of fear.
habitat: Abhartach is associated with burial grounds and the location of his grave. He is said to haunt the area surrounding his burial place, typically a remote and desolate location. He may also be found in dark forests or near sources of fresh blood.
behavior: Abhartach is a malevolent and tyrannical figure. He is driven by a thirst for blood and a desire to exert control over others. He is known to terrorize the local population and demand tribute in the form of blood. He is cunning and ruthless, and will stop at nothing to achieve his goals. He is also prone to fits of rage and violence.

keyMyths.title: The Killing of Abhartach
keyMyths.description: Cathán, a skilled warrior, eventually defeats Abhartach after he repeatedly rises from the grave. Cathán pins him with a stone and plants a rowan tree to prevent his return, ending his reign of terror.
